DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TALAB QARAJAH's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for fraudulent transfer is overruled. Under the UFTA (CC 3439 et seq.) any creditor of the original owner may maintain an action to unwind the transfer. "Civil Code section 3439.075 sets forth the remedies in a fraudulent conveyance action. Under subdivision (a)(1) of that section, a creditor who makes a successful fraudulent conveyance claim may obtain '[a]voidance of the transfer or obligation to the extent necessary to satisfy the creditor's claim.'" Kirkeby v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 642, 648. Here, plaintiff is a creditor within the meaning of the Act per 3439.01(a)(c) because he has a claim as to the corporate assets. The corporation is defunct and the assets should have been distributed to the shareholders. (Par. 53 of the Amended Complaint). The demurrer to the 6th "cause of action" for conspiracy is overruled. It is true that civil conspiracy is not an independent cause of action. See Kidron v. Movie Acquisition Corp. (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1571, 1581 ("As is well established, civil conspiracy is not an independent tort."). Plaintiff should have alleged the conspiracy theory as a general charging allegation and the court treats the allegations as such. Defendant can be liable on the conversion and UFTA claims by participating in the wrongdoing. Defendant owed a duty to refrain from participating in the wrongdoing (committing the torts) and injuring plaintiff's property interests. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1(B) And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brent Kling of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP telephone number 415-626-7744 for Moving Party/Defendant Talab Qarajah.

No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. Counsel for the Defendant stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopted the tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TALAB QARAJAH'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1(B) And Request For Monetary Sanctions is granted as to the following: FI 17.1 as to RFA 1, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 16, 17, 31, 32, 33. The Motion is denied as to the following: FI 17.1 as to RFAs 11 ("holder of record is ambiguous"), 15 ("registered as a shareholder" is ambiguous, 20, 24, 25, 26, 29 (plaintiff answered these interrogatories). The fact that responding party copied factual allegations from his verified complaint does not make the responses to form interrogatory non-responsive. However, references to, for example, "paragraphs 7 through 68" to the Verified First Amended Complaint are not code-compliant responses. Plaintiff must make a good faith to provide clear and straightforward responses to each interrogatory and not simply repeat responses to interrogatories. Moving party's request for sanctions is denied per CCP 2023.040. Responding party's request for sanctions is denied under CCP 2023.040(a) because the Court does not find that moving party engaged in a misuse of the discovery process. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)
